pre: I have some awful memory problems as well, and have to interrupt people RIGHT THEN and if I don't... I will forget what I was going to say.
text: It's awful because I'm convinced everyone thinks I'm just a rude motherfucker, but really if I don't interrupt you I'm going to spend my whole day trying to remember what I was thinking.
post: So either I look like the asshole for being the worst interrupter on the planet, or I get mad at myself and it lasts for hours if it happens repeatedly throughout the day.

pre: Scheduling phone calls should work, in that case.
text: It drives me up the wall to be interrupted because being yanked out of focus is jarring, and I lose the tenuous map I've built up in my mind of what I'm doing, what I'm going to be doing next, and how I'm going to get there, and it takes me 20-30 minutes to build it back up.
post: At work, I had coworkers who'd drop by every so often just to ask quick questions (obviously getting in a bit of exercise as they walked around the building and getting in some social time as well), but I hated that because I'd lose whatever I was working on and not be able to get it back for a long time.
